title: Air-fryer chips
subtitle: Crisp outside, fluffy inside, using very little oil.
excerpt: Potato strips soaked in cold water to remove surface starch, dried, tossed in a teaspoon of oil and cooked at 200°C until golden and crisp. No deep fat, no oil splatter. Forty minutes start to finish. Serves two.
sourceNotes: Air-fryer chip technique; the cold-water soak to remove surface starch is a standard chip-making step documented in British chip-shop practice and Heston Blumenthal's triple-cooked chip method. The air-fryer adaptation is contemporary.

content.text: Cold water, dry, one teaspoon of oil. Those three steps are the difference between crispy air-fryer chips and pale soggy ones. The soak draws out the surface starch that would otherwise steam and stick; the thorough dry allows the outside to crisp. The small amount of oil conducts heat and gives colour.
Use a floury potato: Maris Piper, King Edward, or a Desiree. Waxy varieties like Charlotte won't fluff on the inside.

items.name: Potato
items.prepNote: Maris Piper or King Edward, peeled

content.text: Cut {{potato}} peeled potatoes into even strips, about 1 cm wide. Put in a bowl, cover with cold water, and leave for 10 minutes. Drain and rinse under cold running water until the water runs clear.
Spread the chips on a clean tea towel and pat completely dry. Any surface moisture will create steam in the basket and prevent crisping. Take time here; a 2-minute dry makes a noticeable difference.

Preheat the air fryer to 200°C for 3 minutes. Toss the dry chips in {{rapeseed-oil}} of rapeseed oil until lightly coated. Arrange in one layer in the basket, no piling. Cook for 20 to 22 minutes, shaking the basket twice during cooking, until the chips are golden and crisp at the edges.
Tip into a warm bowl and season with {{sea-salt-fine}} of salt. Serve immediately.

items.fix: Pat completely dry; every drop of moisture matters. Cook in a single layer.
items.cause: Not dried enough, or basket overcrowded.
items.symptom: Chips pale and soft after cooking

items.fix: Shake halfway through. A light oil coating on every piece prevents sticking. Preheat the basket.
items.symptom: Chips stick together in the basket

items.fix: 1 cm is the upper limit. Thicker strips need longer at a lower temperature (180°C for 25 to 28 minutes).
Add salt after cooking, not before; salt draws moisture out during the cook and interferes with crisping.
items.cause: Seasoned before cooking rather than immediately after.

content.text: Air-fryer chips work as a quick weeknight side alongside a burger, grilled chicken or a simple sausage. They won't replace fish-and-chip shop chips in a direct comparison; the texture is lighter and slightly more baked than deep-fried. But for a Tuesday night when you want chips without the oil and the clean-up, they are very good.
